steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1 claims photography's use of lines elevates it to fine art. Sentence 2 goes further, calling linear forms photography's "indisputable and exclusive domain" surpassing even celebrated painters. The second sentence does not contradict or pivot from the first — it doubles down on the same argument. The blank needs a transition that signals reinforcement or amplification.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Indeed,': "Indeed" signals that the second statement reinforces and emphasizes the first — Yalenti's second claim amplifies his first claim about lines and fine art. ✓
  • Choice B — 'In any case,': "In any case" signals dismissal of prior considerations and a pivot to something that holds regardless — no such pivot or dismissal occurs here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'However,': "However" signals contrast or contradiction — but Sentence 2 agrees with and strengthens Sentence 1, not contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Even so,': "Even so" signals concession despite an obstacle — Sentence 1 presents no obstacle for Sentence 2 to overcome. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
